mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over the prior 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and eases financial conditions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns following declines are not significantly higher than baseline (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points within 3 months signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration, prompting equity investors to price in reduced consumer spending and earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive or flat 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the threshold breach, or the effect is not statistically distinguishable from unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment growth decelerates while the federal funds rate remains elevated, tight monetary policy compounds a weakening labor market, jointly signaling a higher probability of recession and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter that exceed the effect of either series alone.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth turns negative while FEDFUNDS remains above its 12-month moving average
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with decelerating labor market
failure_condition: Joint-condition subsample shows forward 60d returns not significantly different from the marginal effect of PAYEMS deceleration alone (interaction term insignificant at p>0.10 in regression).

mechanism_narrative: During a mid-cycle expansion, positive month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects resilient consumer demand, supporting corporate revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; this relationship weakens or reverses in late-cycle or contractionary regimes where strong consumption raises inflation and rate-hike fears.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS growth and forward 20d returns is not positive and significant (p<0.05) specifically within identified mid-cycle expansion windows over a 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and corporate capacity utilization, which positively feeds into near-term earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO month-over-month change and forward 5d returns remains below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI year-over-year inflation rises above a threshold consistent with policy tightening expectations, investors anticipate restrictive monetary policy and multiple compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05) in the subsample where CPI YoY exceeds 4%, compared to the unconditional distribution.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling while the federal funds rate is simultaneously elevated indicates that restrictive monetary policy is actively suppressing interest-rate-sensitive demand, a leading indicator of broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days beyond what either series predicts independently.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 5% over 3 months while FEDFUNDS is above its 24-month moving average
failure_condition: Interaction coefficient between HOUST decline and elevated FEDFUNDS is statistically insignificant (p>0.10) in explaining forward 20d returns relative to a model with additive terms only.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion characterized by unemployment near cyclical lows and strong payroll growth, labor market overheating raises wage-inflation and Fed tightening risk, causing markets to price in future policy restriction and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; in early or mid-cycle regimes the same signal instead reflects healthy growth and is not predictive of negative returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 4% and PAYEMS 6-month annualized growth above 2%
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Within late-cycle tight-labor-market windows, forward 60d SPX returns are not significantly negative (p>0.05) relative to unconditional baseline, or the effect reverses sign across two consecutive out-of-sample late-cycle episodes.
